steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence shows “sessions may ______ repetitive improvisation exercises to refine an actor's natural responses.” This is a transitive verb (“include”) followed by a noun phrase (“repetitive improvisation exercises to refine an actor's natural responses”) that serves as its direct object.

steps.title: Apply the verb-object rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "sessions may [BLANK] repetitive improvisation exercises..." — the blank is a transitive verb slot; "repetitive improvisation exercises" is its direct object. No punctuation separates a verb from its direct object.
  • Choice A — include:: "sessions may include: repetitive improvisation exercises..." — colons can precede lists after a complete clause, but here the colon splits verb from object. ✗
  • Choice B — include: "sessions may include repetitive improvisation exercises..." — verb flows directly into its object with no interruption. ✓
  • Choice C — include;: "sessions may include; repetitive improvisation exercises..." — semicolon between verb and object is never grammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — include,: "sessions may include, repetitive improvisation exercises..." — comma between transitive verb and direct object is not permitted. ✗
That is the correct answer.
